name: Transcode Downloader
description: Adds a quality-selection download button to Jellyfin item detail pages.
overview: Adds a 'Download (Transcode…)' entry to the More menu on every movie and episode detail page. Selecting it opens a quality picker — choose a bitrate tier and Jellyfin re-encodes the video server-side to H.264/AAC, streaming it to your browser with a live progress bar, or pick Original to save the unmodified source file directly. Requires the File Transformation companion plugin (>= v2.2.1.0) for permission-safe script injection.

versions.changelog:
Fix queue panel resetting progress bar when new items are added

Incremental DOM updates replace full re-renders so the active download's
progress bar is preserved when a second item joins the queue.

Add multi-download queue

Downloads now enqueue instead of starting immediately. Each queued item has its own cancel button; cancelling removes only that item and the next starts automatically. The bottom-right panel shows all queued items with live progress for the active one. Queue is in-memory only.
Update logo, rename console prefix to [TranscodeDownloader], fix README and manifest

Fix transcode quality tiers and stale item on first page load

- On first load, quality sheet now awaits metadata before building the
  tier list, so only valid bitrates (below source bitrate) are shown
- Quality sheet now re-derives the item ID from the URL at open time,
  preventing downloads targeting the previous movie when navigating
  between items before the hash-change event fires

Replace dropdown button with action sheet menu entry

- Remove injected select+button from the detail page
- Add 'Download (Transcode…)' entry to Jellyfin's native 'Mehr' menu
- Quality selection via a second overlay in Jellyfin's action sheet style
- Fixes reload-required bug; no longer disrupts the detail page layout

fix: show plugin logo in catalog and installed plugin detail page

- Add imageUrl to manifest.json so the catalog displays the logo
- Include logo.png in the release zip for the installed plugin detail page

rename: QuickDownload → Transcode Downloader + add logo and build CI

- Plugin display name is now "Transcode Downloader"
- All internals renamed to Jellyfin.Plugin.TranscodeDownloader
- Controller route changed to /TranscodeDownloader/ClientScript
- Added logo.png
- Added PR build workflow

Fix: force transcode to honour selected quality tier

Without allowVideoStreamCopy=false Jellyfin could direct-stream the
original file, ignoring MaxStreamingBitrate and MaxHeight entirely.
Downloads now correctly re-encode to the selected resolution and bitrate.

Fix: force transcode to honour selected quality tier

Without allowVideoStreamCopy=false Jellyfin could direct-stream the
original file, ignoring MaxStreamingBitrate and MaxHeight entirely.
Downloads now correctly re-encode to the selected resolution and bitrate.
fix: delay revokeObjectURL to prevent download failure after completion

fix: button appears on first load without page reload

Replace 20x250ms polling retry loop with a MutationObserver that fires
the moment Jellyfin's SPA renders the detail-view container. The old
loop could time out before the SPA finished hydrating, requiring a
manual page reload to see the download button.

fix: use ApiClient.getItem() for metadata fetch to avoid 401

Jellyfin 10.11 rejects api_key query-param auth on the Items endpoint,
causing the dropdown to stay empty. Switch to client.getItem() which
sends the correct Authorization header.

fix: populate dropdown after UI injection to eliminate race condition

The metadata fetch often completed before Jellyfin rendered the detail
container, so populateDropdown() exited early (select === null) without
ever setting currentItem. Clicking Download then silently did nothing.

- Move currentItem assignment before the early return in populateDropdown()
- Re-call populateDropdown() after injecting the UI if data already arrived
- Reset currentItem when navigating to a new item to avoid stale options

fix: strip Accept-Encoding/Content-Encoding to prevent compressed-body corruption

Jellyfin's static file middleware serves index.html pre-compressed when the
client advertises Accept-Encoding. The IStartupFilter middleware was buffering
those compressed bytes and writing them back with Content-Encoding: gzip still
set, causing browsers to report 'cannot decode raw data'.

Fix: remove Accept-Encoding from the request before intercepting so upstream
never activates compression; also strip Content-Encoding from the response.

fix: inject script tag via middleware — button now appears without companion plugins

The script tag is now injected by an ASP.NET Core IStartupFilter that
intercepts index.html responses in Jellyfin's HTTP pipeline. No File
Transformation plugin required, no disk writes, no timing sensitivity.

Also fixes plugin loading error in the admin UI (Plugin constructor now
calls SetAttributes).

fix: call SetAttributes in Plugin constructor so CanUninstall doesn't throw

BasePlugin (non-generic) never calls SetAttributes — only BasePlugin<T> does
in its own constructor. Without it, AssemblyFilePath stays null, and
CanUninstall throws NullReferenceException when GET /Plugins serializes the
plugin list, breaking the admin plugins page on every page load.

Fix: inject IApplicationPaths and call SetAttributes the same way BasePlugin<T>
does, giving Jellyfin a valid assembly path and data folder path.

Serve client script from controller and inject in-memory

- QuickDownloadController serves the embedded plugin.js at GET /QuickDownload/ClientScript (AllowAnonymous) so the web root never needs to be writable
- WebAssetInjector registers an in-memory File Transformation callback instead of patching index.html on disk; survives Jellyfin web updates and is auto-removed on plugin uninstall
- On-disk fallback retained for installs without File Transformation, with a loud actionable log recommending FT >= v2.2.1.0
- Base-path-aware script src for Jellyfin deployments behind a sub-path

Cache-bust plugin.js on injection

Add a ?v={assembly version} query to the injected plugin.js script tag so
browsers reload the script after a plugin upgrade instead of serving a stale
cached copy. First release cut via the new fully tag-driven release pipeline.
